channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faces criticism for speech to Navy sailors during Singapore visit
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth drew widespread criticism after delivering a brief motivational speech to Navy sailors during a visit aboard the USS Boxer, which was docked in Singapore. The appea...

item.title: Jinggoy Estrada secures bail in graft case involving flood control projects
item.description: Former Senator Jinggoy Estrada has posted bail in connection with a graft case related to flood control projects in the Philippines. The case involves allegations of misuse and irregularities in gover...

item.title: Top 7 Films and Series to Stream This Weekend Across Netflix and Prime Video
item.description: Dis weekend no be small one for people wey like stream correct films and series. For Prime Video, 'Spider-Noir' don drop, wey be live-action Marvel series set for 1930s New York. E dey follow Ben Reil...

item.title: World Cup 2026 and the shift in Nigerian football betting habits
item.description: Football and betting dey hand in hand for Nigeria, and World Cup 2026 go bring bigger changes. The tournament go dey hosted for USA, Canada and Mexico, with more teams, matches, and better digital cov...

item.title: Man extradited from United States after fleeing Canada to avoid prison sentence for child exploitation conviction
item.description: A man who left Canada in an effort to avoid serving a prison sentence for a child exploitation conviction has been extradited back from the United States, according to reporting from Vancouver. The in...

item.title: Canadian economy shows modest decline amid trade uncertainties
item.description: Canada’s economy experienced a slight contraction in the first quarter of 2026, with annualized GDP falling 0.1 per cent, following a 1-per-cent decline in the fourth quarter of 2025. While two consec...

item.title: TikTok creator Monique Pearls passes away after brief illness, leaves young son and family in mourning
item.description: Popular TikTok content creator Monica Oma Owunaka, widely known as Monique Pearls, has sadly passed away after a short illness, leaving behind her husband and a one-year-old son. According to a family...

item.title: Henry Mwinuka leads RSSB Tigers to BAL final, marking a historic milestone for Tanzanian coaching
item.description: Henry Mwinuka, a seasoned Tanzanian basketball coach, stands on the cusp of a historic achievement as his Rwanda-based RSSB Tigers prepare for the BAL final against Angola’s Petro de Luanda in Kigali....

item.title: Spurious liquor claims 15 lives in Pune and Pimpri-Chinchwad; eight arrested
item.description: Over the past three days, at least fifteen people have tragically lost their lives in Pune and Pimpri-Chinchwad after consuming spurious liquor, authorities confirmed. Police have arrested eight indiv...

item.title: Full lineup of global artists announced for 2026 FIFA World Cup ceremonies and final show
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is set to be one of the most entertainment-packed editions in the tournament’s history, with FIFA unveiling a massive lineup of international music stars scheduled to perform a...

item.title: KZN police on high alert ahead of planned trucking protest and possible road blockades
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal police have placed themselves on high alert ahead of a planned national shutdown linked to the All Truck Drivers Forum SA (ATDFSA), which has called for action against the employment of ...

item.title: Chris McCullough needed more time to find rhythm in PBA, says ABS-CBN report
item.description: ABS-CBN News reported on May 29, 2026, that Chris McCullough, a player in the Philippine Basketball Association (PBA), indicated he only needed additional time to settle into his rhythm with his team....

item.title: Remote Philippine schools expand access to internet and solar power under DepEd-ADB program
item.description: Twenty-one geographically isolated schools across the Philippines have been equipped with satellite internet, solar-powered facilities, tablets, and digitized learning materials through a P112-million...

item.title: Amanyanabo of Koniju Endorses Port Harcourt Arsenal Community and Promises Support
item.description: The Amanyanabo of Koniju town in Okrika Local Government Area of Rivers State, His Royal Majesty King Winston Odiye Alamina Oputibeya XII, has shown him strong support for the Port Harcourt Arsenal Co...

item.title: Masasi residents to receive free specialist treatment during Mkapa memorial medical outreach camp
item.description: Residents of Masasi in Mtwara Region are set to benefit from free specialist medical services as doctors from the Benjamin Mkapa Referral Hospital begin a medical outreach camp aimed at improving acce...

item.title: POPIA and PAIA Compliance in South Africa: A Human Rights Imperative for Businesses
item.description: This opinion piece argues that POPIA and PAIA are not mere regulatory requirements but constitutional tools that protect privacy and transparency as human rights in South Africa. It stresses that comp...

item.title: Mbeki Challenges TRC Inquiry Summons Amid NPA Political Interference U-Turn
item.description: In a significant development over the past week, South Africa's National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) appears to have retracted its earlier admission of political interference in prosecutions linked to...

item.title: Kenneth Law admits guilt to multiple assisted suicide charges as murder charges are dropped
item.description: Kenneth Law has pleaded guilty to 14 criminal charges related to assisting suicide, according to court proceedings reported in Toronto. As part of the legal resolution, prosecutors indicated that the ...

item.title: Three people, including a child, die after fall from south London tower block
item.description: Three people, including a child, died after falling from a high-rise residential building in Elephant and Castle, south London, on Wednesday morning. Emergency services were called to Churchyard Row a...

item.title: Mudavadi Says Kenya Is Building 16 Ebola Preparedness Centres to Strengthen National Health Response
item.description: Prime Cabinet Secretary Musalia Mudavadi has defended the planned construction of a US-linked Ebola preparedness facility in Nanyuki, saying it is part of a wider government strategy to establish 16 s...
